content.full_text:
Local No. 188

International Association of Fire Fighters

AFFILIATIONS:
AFL-CIO
CALIFORNIA FEDERATION OF LABOR
FEDERATED FIRE FIGHTERS OF CALIF.
CENTRAL LABOR COUNCIL OF CONTRA
COSTA COUNTY
C.O.P.E.

3530 MORNINGSIDE DRIVE

RICHMOND, CALIFORNIA 94803

Telephone: 223-1342

State of California
Fair Political Practice
Legal Division
Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Council
428 J Street
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

I would like to request a formal position from the
California Fair Political Practice Commission on two separate
activities I am developing.

One activity is the use of an educational brochure
that may be used by interested parties to define important
issues in an election and to educate the voters on those
issues. The brochure may also make recommendations on how
these important issues may be addressed and may be critical
or supportive of policies taken by local governments. The
education brochure would not at any time recommend a vote for
or against any candidate, nor would it ever name any candidate
for an elective office.

The question that I have with respect to the above
educational brochure is: would the source of funds used to
print and publish this brochure have to be filed with the FPPC?

I am sending to your office a copy of an educational
brochure that I developed and mailed to 35,000 households in
the 1990 East Bay Municipal Utility District's election. This
brochure should give you a better understanding of what I have
in mind and what I plan to do with such a brochure.

If possible I would like an immediate response from
your office on this issue, because I want the brochure to go
out to the public before the election efforts get under way.

The second activity that I am planning is: raising
money for our Union of which I am the President. The Union in
question is the Richmond Firefighters Union Local 188, in
the City of Richmond California. At the present time, funds
for operating the Union comes principally from the Union dues
of our 115 members by a payroll deduction process. In addition


page 2

to the Union Dues, there is a $10.00 per month assessment on
all members to develop additional funds for the Union. All
of these funds that are raised are placed into our general
fund account which is used for operation of our Union.
Some of the funds from our general account are placed into
our two Political Action Committees from which the Union
supports or opposes candidates and Measures.

The new activity the Union is comtemplating, and
for which we are seeking your legal opinion, is to raise
funds from businesses and others that support the daily
activities of the Richmond Firefighters. These "new funds"
would be deposited into a seperate newly established account.
The funds from this new account would be used to pay for the
every day operating expenses of the Union, i.e., lawyers,
Union Officer's salaries and expenses, dues payments to parent
labor organizations, blood bank account, etc. Not any of the
money from this "new account" would be used for political
activities, nor, would the money be targeted in any way for
political activities. However, I should point out to you
that in raising these "new monies", the Union will have more
money to spend for political activities taken from the general
fund account. Since part, or all, of our day to day operating
expenses will be paid out of our "new account". Therefore, in
this respect, the "new money" will impact our political activi-
ties simply by "freeing up" our membership dues and assessment
monies for greater political activities.

I spoke with Mr. Elliot Block regarding this issue
before he left your office and he felt that under Section 18215,
(Regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission, Title 2,
Division of the California Code of Regulations) that as long
as "new monies" raised were not in anyway co-mingled, or ear-
marked for any political activity, then it would not be necessary
to report those contributions made to the Union and used solely
for Union operational activities, and not at any time used for
political purposes. Mr. Block suggested that I request the
legal opinion in writing, since a verbal opinion would not be
binding.

Therefore, I am requesting a written legal opinion on
this issue; If our Union raises "new monies" from businesses,
fund raisers, etc. for Union operating expenses and placed the
"new monies" into a new separate account which would never at
anytime be intermingled with political activities or no political
activity money, but used solely for day to day operating expenses
of our Union, would it be necessary to report those funds to
the FPPC?

page 3

I have posed two questions for your legal opinion:
On the first question, The need to report contributions on
an educational brochure which does not name any candidates or
Measures, I would like an immediate reply if possible.

On the second question, The need to report contributions to our Union if the funds are not used at any time or in
any way for political activities, this response may be made
at anytime that is convenient to you within the 21 day period
which is allowed.

Thank you for your time and consideration of these
issues.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Darrell L. Reese, Pres.
Local 188, IAFF
Mailing Addr: 117 3rd Street
Rodeo, Ca. 94572

encl: 1                                (510)799-1313

The image provided appears to be almost entirely blank or extremely faint. I can see only scattered dots or marks across a white/light gray background, but no legible text content is discernible enough to transcribe accurately.

If this document contains text, it may be:
- Too faint to read in this scan
- Heavily degraded
- Requiring enhancement or a higher quality image

Could you please provide a clearer or higher contrast version of the document?
